message:
fix(id): length-prefix ID seed parts to eliminate delimiter-position collisions

`generate_id_seed` concatenates title / description / creator / timestamp
/ nonce using a bare `|` separator. Because the user-controlled fields
(title, description, creator) can themselves contain `|`, two logically
distinct issues could hash to the same seed:

    seed("a",   Some("b"), ...)  →  "a|b|..."
    seed("a|b", None,       ...) →  "a|b|..."

This is a classic delimiter-collision bug. It's low-probability under
normal use, but deterministic ID generation must be injective over its
inputs — otherwise concurrent creators can accidentally produce the
same ID and the existence-check retry loop can't distinguish true
duplicates from input-domain collisions.

- Rewrite the seed as a sequence of length-prefixed parts via a private
  `append_seed_part` helper: each part is encoded as `"<len>:<value>"`
  so the byte length of each field is committed to the seed before its
  contents. Two inputs that differ only in where a `|` sits will now
  differ in their length prefixes and therefore in the final hash.
- Stringify `timestamp` and `nonce` up front so the same encoding path
  handles all five parts uniformly.
- Add a regression test (`id_seed_distinguishes_delimiter_positions`)
  asserting `seed("a", Some("b"), ..)` and `seed("a|b", None, ..)`
  produce different seeds at the same timestamp / nonce.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
